entries.label: Basic thread
entries.note: spawn returns JoinHandle; join() waits for the thread to finish

entries.label: Move closure
entries.note: Use move to transfer ownership of captured variables to the thread

entries.label: Return value from thread
entries.note: join() returns Result<T> where T is the closure's return type

entries.label: Named threads
entries.note: Builder allows setting name and stack size

entries.label: Scoped threads
entries.note: Scoped threads can borrow from the parent stack; joined automatically

entries.label: Available parallelism
entries.note: Query the number of hardware threads available

entries.label: mpsc channel
entries.note: Multiple producer, single consumer; send/recv for communication

entries.label: Multiple producers
entries.note: Clone the sender for multiple producers; drop original to close channel

entries.label: Sync channel
entries.note: Bounded channel; send blocks when buffer is full

entries.label: try_recv
entries.note: Non-blocking receive; returns immediately with TryRecvError if empty

entries.label: recv_timeout
entries.note: Block up to a specified duration waiting for a message

entries.label: Basic Mutex
entries.note: lock() returns MutexGuard; auto-releases when guard is dropped

entries.label: Shared Mutex with Arc
entries.note: Arc provides shared ownership; Mutex provides mutual exclusion

entries.label: Poisoned Mutex
entries.note: A Mutex becomes poisoned if a thread panics while holding the lock

entries.label: try_lock
entries.note: Non-blocking attempt to acquire the lock

entries.label: Read/write lock
entries.note: Multiple readers OR one writer; more efficient when reads dominate

entries.label: Shared RwLock
entries.note: Arc + RwLock for concurrent read-heavy shared state

entries.label: try_read / try_write
entries.note: Non-blocking attempts; useful to avoid deadlocks

entries.label: AtomicBool
entries.note: Lock-free boolean; use for flags and simple synchronization

entries.label: AtomicUsize counter
entries.note: Lock-free counter; faster than Mutex for simple operations

entries.label: Compare and swap
entries.note: Atomically set value only if current matches expected; CAS operation

entries.label: Ordering explained
entries.note: SeqCst is safest default; Relaxed is fastest but weakest guarantee

entries.label: Send
entries.note: Compiler checks Send bounds at compile time; prevents data races

entries.label: Sync
entries.note: Sync means safe to reference from multiple threads simultaneously

entries.label: Auto-derived
entries.note: Compiler derives automatically; manual impl requires unsafe and great care

entries.label: Parallel iterator
entries.note: Drop-in replacement for iterators; automatically parallelizes

entries.label: Parallel map/filter
entries.note: Same API as regular iterators; work distributed across threads

entries.label: par_sort
entries.note: Parallel sort; significantly faster for large collections

entries.label: Custom thread pool
entries.note: Configure the number of threads; install runs closures on the pool

entries.label: Thread pool with channels
entries.note: Fan-out work to threads; collect results via channel

entries.label: Barrier
entries.note: Synchronization point; all threads wait until everyone arrives

entries.label: Once / OnceLock
entries.note: Initialize exactly once; thread-safe; great for lazy global state

entries.label: Condvar
entries.note: Wait for a condition to become true; pairs with Mutex
